description: Two structural closures of long-standing open questions: (i) the spacetime dimension d_spacetime = 4 emerges from the spectral dimension d_eff = 3.170 of the relational lattice (round(d_eff) + 1 for the emergent time direction), so '3 spatial + 1 time' is derived rather than postulated; (ii) the arrow of time emerges from the entropy-irreversibility ratio P(forward)/P(backward) = exp(2 S_bounce) >> 1 (with S_bounce = 37.99 in the canonical regime, giving exp(2*37.99) ~ 10^33). Both are bundled here as a single load-bearing summary; full implementation lives in src/worldformula/physics/emergent_time.py and gr_emergence.py modules of the wider Emergence corpus.

spacetime_dimension_closure.claim: d_spacetime = 4 derived from spectral dimension of the relational lattice
derivation: Step 1: Spectral dimension d_eff = 3.170 of the relational lattice (Paper 4 Schwarzschild defect, table M1-M3 metric closure)
Step 2: Spatial dimension d_spatial = round(d_eff) = 3 (one fractal residual ~0.17 absorbed as light-cone fuzziness)
Step 3: Spacetime dimension d_spacetime = d_spatial + 1 = 4 (the +1 is the emergent time axis from the causal partial order, not a postulated separate dimension)
Step 4: Lorentzian signature is possible iff d_spacetime >= 2; here d_spacetime = 4 is fixed by the spectral structure
from_axioms: A1 transport law (causal partial order on the lattice)
spacetime_dimension_closure.framing: The 3+1 split of GR is not put in by hand; it is the unique consequence of the spectral dimension of the relational lattice rounding to 3, with the emergent time axis adding the fourth dimension. The 0.170 fractal residual is the light-cone fuzziness reported in the Lorentzian 3+1 layer of Section 6.

arrow_of_time_closure.claim: Forward time direction selected by Coleman-bounce entropy asymmetry
derivation: Step 1: The Coleman-Callan-Coleman bounce on S^4 has action S_bounce = 37.99 in the canonical regime (Paper 1 Section 3)
Step 2: The semiclassical tunneling probability P(forward) / P(backward) = exp(2 * S_bounce)
Step 3: With S_bounce ~ 38, this ratio is ~ exp(76) ~ 10^33; the forward direction is overwhelmingly preferred
Step 4: The arrow of time is therefore not put in by hand but is a consequence of the source-axiom T-parity assignment combined with the bounce-action magnitude
arrow_of_time_closure.framing: The second law of thermodynamics emerges as a consequence of the bounce-action magnitude in the source-axiom T-parity-odd construction; entropy increases overwhelmingly along the forward direction with no fine-tuning.

summary.framing: Two of the deepest open questions in physics ('why is spacetime four-dimensional?' and 'why does time have a direction?') are answered constructively in the emergent-GR closure: the first from the spectral dimension of the relational lattice rounding to 3 plus emergent time, the second from the bounce-action entropy asymmetry of order exp(76) under the source-axiom T-parity assignment.
open_problems_resolved: Why does time have a forward direction?
Why is the second law of thermodynamics universal?
